close_reason: Defensive code: unreachable branch is correct safety measure
description:
src/lib.rs lines 66-76 in map_get_error handle ParameterVersionNotFoundException, returning Backend (with a comment explaining why it is NOT NotFound — the parameter still exists, only the requested version is gone).

This branch is currently unreachable in practice: the code calls `get_parameter().name(...).with_decryption(true)` and never `.version(...)`. The AWS SDK only emits ParameterVersionNotFoundException when an explicit version is requested.

Verdict: KEEP. Rationale:
1. The branch is correct if a future change adds version pinning — without it, a version-not-found would fall through to Unavailable (transient), which would be wrong (the requested version is permanently gone).
2. Removing it requires the next contributor who adds .version() to remember to add the branch back. That's a footgun.
3. Cost is one if-block; benefit is forward-correctness.

This issue exists for the audit trail so a future reviewer who flags 'dead branch' can be pointed here.
title: secretx-aws-ssm: is_parameter_version_not_found branch is currently unreachable but defensively correct — keep
